title: FMEA Sample — IR Sensor Encoder (Canonical Course Example)

source.course: Cornell CESYS527 — Identifying and Evaluating Risk
source.context: Pedal-Motor-Bicycle (PMB) design — IR Sensor Encoder subsystem

description: Machine-readable transcription of the course-provided FMEA sample. Use this as the calibration reference for format, rating assignment, corrective-action quality, and stoplight chart construction. The 7 cause rows are distributed across 3 failure modes (F.1, F.2, F.3) with two rounds of corrective actions and three stoplight charts (before, after round 1, after round 2).

system_context.subsystem_function: Reports number of wheel rotations to the Demand Algorithm, which determines energy usage vs. storage for the motor-assist system.

severity.assignment_rule: Assign the WORST (highest) severity rating that has at least ONE condition met by the effect/cause.

levels.label: Negligible
conditions: Cost is less than 5% of the total budget
Requires less than 1 day to address
Performance is adversely affected by less than 5%
No harm to a person

levels.label: Marginal
conditions: Cost is 5%-10% of the total budget
Requires 1-6 days to address
Performance is adversely affected by 5-10%
No harm to a person

levels.label: Critical
conditions: Cost is 10%-20% of the total budget
Requires 1-2 weeks to address
Performance is adversely affected by 10-20%
No harm to a person

levels.label: Catastrophic
conditions: Cost is more than 20% of the total budget
Requires more than 2 weeks to address
Performance is adversely affected by greater than 20%
Causes potential harm to a person

likelihood.maturity_context: Prototype development. In a final product, all of these levels would be considered too frequent.

levels.label: Remote
conditions: Occurs less than once every 3 months

levels.label: Unlikely
conditions: Occurs between once every 2 weeks to once every 3 months

levels.label: Possible
conditions: Occurs no more than once a week

levels.label: Likely
conditions: Occurs greater than once a week
Occurs in 5-10% of the operations

levels.label: Frequent
conditions: Occurs greater than once a week
Occurs in greater than 10% of the operations

failure_modes.failure_mode: Reported number of rotations from the IR sensor is too few
failure_effects: Energy usage is larger than necessary
Bicycle goes far faster than desired

cause_rows.possible_cause: sensor detector or emitter is partially blocked or damaged
cause_rows.corrective_action: Add a shielding cone to the detector. Add a sweep to the emitter just before it passes the detector to help clear off the emitter. Increase the intensity of the emitter. Research more robust parts. Put in a redundant encoder and adjust sensor filter system to account for this redundancy. Ensure manual override system is functioning properly.
cause_rows.corrective_action_effort: 15 hrs. (See Timeline's IR Sensor Risk Plan B)
cause_rows.troubleshooting: Check encoders to make sure they are securely fastened and properly aligned. Check encoders for debris and clean if need be. Check encoders to make sure they're still working. Replace encoders if need be.

cause_rows.possible_cause: sensor detector or emitter is damaged
cause_rows.corrective_action: Research more robust parts. Put in a redundant encoder and adjust sensor filter system to account for this redundancy. Ensure manual override system is functioning properly.
cause_rows.corrective_action_effort: 12 hrs. (See Timeline's IR Sensor Risk Plan B)
cause_rows.troubleshooting: Check encoders to make sure they are securely fastened and properly aligned. Check encoders for debris and clean if need be. Check encoders to make sure they're still working. Replace encoders if need be.

cause_rows.possible_cause: sensor detector or emitter has become loose or is no longer oriented properly
cause_rows.corrective_action: Increase the robustness of the mechanical housing. Include checking its secureness as part of the recommended start up safety check. Put in a redundant encoder and adjust sensor filter system to account for this redundancy.
cause_rows.corrective_action_effort: 12 hrs. (See Timeline's IR Sensor Risk Plan B and Plan C)
cause_rows.troubleshooting: Check encoders to make sure they are securely fastened and properly aligned. Check wiring for frays and proper connections. Replace encoders or connections if need be.

failure_modes.failure_mode: Reported number of rotations from the IR sensor is too many
failure_effects: Energy storage is conserved or even increased more than it should be
Bicycle goes far slower than desired

cause_rows.possible_cause: sensor detector or emitter is partially damaged
cause_rows.corrective_action: Use COTS Encoder. Put in a redundant encoder and adjust sensor filter system to account for this redundancy. Ensure manual override system is functioning properly.

adjusted_round_1.note: Cause was already LOW risk; corrective action applied as precaution.

cause_rows.corrective_action_effort: 10 hrs. (See Timeline's IR Sensor Risk Plan E)
cause_rows.troubleshooting: Check encoders to make sure they are securely fastened and properly aligned. Check encoders for debris and clean if need be. Check encoders to make sure they're still working. Replace encoders if need be.

failure_effects: Controller switches to energy storage only, no usage
Bicycle functions inefficiently until user disengages PMB engine

cause_rows.corrective_action: Research more robust parts. Put in a redundant encoder and adjust sensor filter system to account for this redundancy.
cause_rows.corrective_action_effort: 12 hrs. (See Timeline's IR Sensor Risk Plan B)
cause_rows.troubleshooting: Check encoders to make sure they are securely fastened and properly aligned. Check wiring for frays and proper connections. Replace encoders or connections if need be.

cause_rows.corrective_action: Increase the robustness of the mechanical housing to take stress of cable connections.
cause_rows.corrective_action_effort: 3 hrs. (See Timeline's IR Sensor Risk Plan D)
cause_rows.troubleshooting: Check encoders to make sure they are securely fastened and properly aligned. Check wiring for frays and proper connections. Replace encoders or connections if need be.

cause_rows.corrective_action: Increase the robustness of the mechanical housing to take stress of cable connections.
cause_rows.corrective_action_effort: 3 hrs. (See Timeline's IR Sensor Risk Plan D)
cause_rows.troubleshooting: Check encoders to make sure they are securely fastened and properly aligned. Check wiring for frays and proper connections. Replace encoders or connections if need be.

stoplight_charts.note: Cells show the COUNT of cause-rows at each (Likelihood, Severity) intersection. Total must equal 7 (total cause rows) across all three charts.

after_corrective_actions_round_2.note: Second iteration (Step 13). F.1 cause 1 received additional corrective action reducing adjusted likelihood from 2 to 1.
